mapping.a_element: emergent universal (near-maximum-entropy) quantum state ensemble on a subsystem
mapping.b_element: maximum-entropy / entropic-inference probability density of log price

mapping.a_element: probability distribution over projective measurement outcomes
mapping.b_element: probability distribution of price changes across the continuous and jump channels

brief_justification: The overlap is only the generic invocation of entropy/probability distributions, not shared transferable machinery. Paper A's mechanism is measurement-induced entanglement teleportation in locally-interacting random-unitary quantum dynamics, with a logarithmic causal-light-cone timescale governing deep thermalisation; Paper B's mechanism is maximum-entropy entropic inference that derives a classical Merton jump-diffusion process (Kolmogorov-Feller / Fokker-Planck equations, Esscher transform, Black-Scholes limit). There is no role-for-role correspondence between a governing quantum-measurement/entanglement law and a maximum-entropy-derived stochastic PDE for asset prices, so the machinery differs.
